title: Exposure and Post-Process

video.note: Covers auto exposure, a hut/dark-space test, manual exposure, post-process volume settings, bloom, vignette, saturation, and Infinite Extent Unbound.

learningTarget: I can use a post-process volume to control exposure and camera-based visual effects in an Unreal scene.

bellRinger.prompt: Why does a camera or your eyes sometimes need time to adjust when moving from a bright place to a dark place?

vocabulary.definition: How bright or dark the rendered scene appears to the camera.
A camera behavior that automatically adjusts brightness, similar to eyes adapting between dark and bright areas.
An Unreal volume that applies camera and image effects after the scene is rendered.
A glow effect around bright areas of the image.
A darkening or shading effect around the edges of the image.
The strength or intensity of colors in the image.
A Post Process Volume setting that makes the effect apply to the entire level instead of only inside the volume.
The exposure measurement mode used to decide how scene brightness should be handled.

exitTicket: What setting allows a post-process volume to affect the whole level?
